message:
Fix thinking-level fallback on header-less sessions, model-switch gating, and session ID repair

Thinking-level sync (src/agent.rs):
- When a session header omits the thinking level, fall back to the
  current runtime thinking level instead of silently returning early.
  This ensures switching to a non-reasoning model correctly clamps
  thinking to Off rather than leaving it stale at the previous level.
- Refactored persist logic: only write back to the session store when
  the effective thinking level actually differs from the current one,
  avoiding redundant header mutations and spurious ThinkingLevelChange
  session entries.
- Added test: sync_runtime_selection_from_session_header_clamps_current_
  thinking_when_header_omits_it — verifies that loading a plain (non-
  reasoning) model from a session with no thinking header clamps to Off,
  persists the clamped value, and emits exactly one change entry.

Model-switch gating (src/interactive/commands.rs):
- Removed the sync_model guard that prevented model application when
  the user hadn't explicitly toggled model sync. The runtime should
  always reconcile to the target entry when it doesn't already match,
  regardless of how the target was selected.
- Collapsed redundant (None, None) match arm into the existing wildcard.

Session save resilience (src/session.rs):
- Moved header.validate() to run after path resolution and filename
  generation, so the validator sees the fully-resolved state.
- Before validation, detect and repair empty/whitespace-only session
  IDs by generating a fresh UUID. This prevents panics on legacy or
  manually-corrupted session files that previously passed save but
  would now fail the stricter validation added in 0fb3cb85.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
